 ### 3. Check for existing Directory.Build.props
 If one exists at the repo/solution root, you'll add properties there. If not, create one.
 
 ---
 
 ## Phase 2 — Enable MTP Runner (all projects at once)
 
 > **Critical**: Do not migrate partially — mixed VSTest/MTP in the same solution is unsupported.
 
 ### 4. Update Directory.Build.props
 Add the following properties, conditioned to match your test project naming convention:
 
 ```xml
 <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
   <!-- Enable MTP runner for MSTest -->
   <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
   <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
 
   <!-- .NET 8/9: bridge dotnet test to MTP -->
   <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
 </PropertyGroup>
 ```
 
 Adjust the `Condition` to match your actual naming convention. Do **not** use `IsTestProject` — it's not available when `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated.
 
 ### 5. Install MTP extension NuGet packages
 Add these to each test project (or via `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management):
 
 | Package | Version | Replaces |
 |---------|---------|----------|
 | `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` | 1.6.2 | `--logger trx` |
 | `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` | 17.13.0 | `--collect "Code Coverage"` |
 
 Only add if you use the feature:
 | Package | Version | Replaces |
 |---------|---------|----------|
 | `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` | 1.6.2 | `--blame-crash` |
 | `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HangDump` | 1.6.2 | `--blame-hang` |
 
 ### 6. Build and verify
 Run `dotnet build` — must produce zero errors.
 
 ---
 
 ## Phase 3 — Update CLI Arguments
 
 ### 7. Translate dotnet test arguments
 Since you're on .NET 8, MTP arguments go after `--`:
 
 | Before (VSTest) | After (MTP on .NET 8) |
 |------------------|-----------------------|
 | `dotnet test --logger trx` | `dotnet test -- --report-trx` |
 | `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage` |
 | `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage;Format=cobertura"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
 | `dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit"` | `dotnet test -- --filter "Category=Unit"` |
 | `dotnet test --blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `dotnet test -- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` |
 | `dotnet test --results-directory <DIR>` | `dotnet test --results-directory <DIR>` (build arg — stays before `--`) |
 
 > **Note**: `--filter` syntax is unchanged for MSTest on MTP. Build args (`-c`, `--no-build`, `--results-directory`, etc.) stay before `--`.
 
 ---
 
 ## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps Pipeline
 
 ### 8. Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2
 
 **Before:**
 ```yaml
 - task: VSTest@3
   inputs:
     testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
     runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
     codeCoverageEnabled: true
 ```
 
 **After:**
 ```yaml
 - task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
   displayName: 'Run tests'
   inputs:
     command: 'test'
     projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
     arguments: >-
       --no-build --configuration Release
       -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
       --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
 ```
 
 ### 9. Update test result publishing (if separate)
 If you publish TRX results with `PublishTestResults@2`, point it at the MTP output directory:
 ```yaml
 - task: PublishTestResults@2
   inputs:
     testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
     testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
 ```
 
 ### 10. Update code coverage publishing
 If using `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2`, point at the Cobertura output from MTP coverage.
 
 ---
 
 ## Phase 5 — Handle Behavioral Differences
 
 ### 11. Zero-tests exit code
 MTP returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest silently succeeds). If any project conditionally runs zero tests, either:
 - Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the test command, or
 - Add to `Directory.Build.props`:
   ```xml
   <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
   ```
 
 ---
 
 ## Phase 6 — Clean Up & Verify
 
 ### 12. Remove VSTest-only packages (optional but recommended)
 From each test project, remove:
 - `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for MTP
 - `MSTest.TestAdapter` — not needed for MTP (the runner is built-in with `EnableMSTestRunner`)
 
 > Keep these if you need a rollback path during transition.
 
 ### 13. Final verification checklist
 - [ ] `dotnet build` — zero errors
 - [ ] `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match baseline from Step 1
 - [ ] Run test executable directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe` — works
 - [ ] CI pipeline produces TRX files
 - [ ] CI pipeline produces code coverage reports
 - [ ] No `vstest.console.exe` or `VSTest@3` references remain in pipeline
 - [ ] Visual Studio / VS Code Test Explorer discovers and runs tests
 
 ---
 
 ## Rollback
 To revert: remove `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` from `Directory.Build.props`, restore `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and revert pipeline to `VSTest@3`.
 
 ## References
 - [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
 - [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
 - [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)

# VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform Migration Plan

## Context
- **5 MSTest test projects** using MSTest 3.8.0 (NuGet packages), Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
- **.NET 8 SDK** — requires `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` + `--` separator for MTP args
- **CI/CD**: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage
- MSTest 3.8.0 ≥ 3.2.0 minimum → no MSTest version upgrade needed

---

## Phase 1 — Baseline & Preparation

### 1. Record baseline test counts
Run `dotnet test` on the current solution and record the total pass/fail/skip counts per project. You'll compare against these after migration.

### 2. Identify test project naming convention
Check whether all 5 test projects follow a consistent naming pattern (e.g., `*.Tests`, `*Tests`, `*.Test`). This determines the `Directory.Build.props` condition.

### 3. Check for existing Directory.Build.props
If one exists at the repo/solution root, you'll add properties there. If not, create one.

---

## Phase 2 — Enable MTP Runner (all 5 projects at once)

> **Critical**: Do not migrate partially — mixed VSTest/MTP in the same solution is unsupported.

### 4. Update Directory.Build.props
Add the following properties, conditioned to match your test project naming convention:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Adjust the `Condition` to match your actual naming convention (e.g., `.Contains('Test')`).
Do **not** use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"` — it's not available when `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated.

### 5. Install MTP extension NuGet packages
Add to each test project (or centrally via `Directory.Packages.props` if using CPM):

| Package | Version | Replaces |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` | 1.6.2 | `--logger trx` |
| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` | 17.13.0 | `--collect "Code Coverage"` |

Only add if you use the feature:
| Package | Version | Replaces |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` | 1.6.2 | `--blame-crash` |
| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.HangDump` | 1.6.2 | `--blame-hang` |

### 6. Build and verify locally
- `dotnet build` — zero errors
- `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match baseline

---

## Phase 3 — Update CLI Arguments

### 7. Translate dotnet test arguments
On .NET 8 SDK, MTP arguments go **after** `--`:

| Before (VSTest) | After (MTP on .NET 8) |
|------------------|-----------------------|
| `dotnet test --logger trx` | `dotnet test -- --report-trx` |
| `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage` |
| `dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage;Format=cobertura"` | `dotnet test -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit"` | `dotnet test -- --filter "Category=Unit"` |
| `dotnet test --blame-crash` | `dotnet test -- --crashdump` |
| `dotnet test --blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `dotnet test -- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` |
| `dotnet test --results-directory <DIR>` | `dotnet test --results-directory <DIR>` (build arg — before `--`) |

> `--filter` syntax is unchanged for MSTest. Build args (`-c`, `--no-build`, `--results-directory`) stay before `--`.

---

## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### 8. Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2

**Before:**
```yaml
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
```

**After:**
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --no-build --configuration Release
      -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

### 9. Update test result publishing (if separate)
If you publish TRX results with `PublishTestResults@2`, point it at the MTP output directory:
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
```

### 10. Update code coverage publishing
If using `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2`, point at the Cobertura output from MTP coverage.

---

## Phase 5 — Handle Behavioral Differences

### 11. Zero-tests exit code
MTP returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest silently succeeds). If any project conditionally runs zero tests, either:
- Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the test command, or
- Add to `Directory.Build.props`:
  ```xml
  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
  ```

---

## Phase 6 — Clean Up & Verify

### 12. Remove VSTest-only packages (after CI is green)
From each test project, remove:
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for MTP
- `MSTest.TestAdapter` — not needed (runner is built-in via `EnableMSTestRunner`)

> Keep these temporarily if you want a rollback path.

### 13. Final verification checklist
- [ ] `dotnet build` — zero errors
- [ ] `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match baseline from Step 1
- [ ] Run test exe directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe` — works
- [ ] CI pipeline produces TRX files
- [ ] CI pipeline produces code coverage reports
- [ ] No `vstest.console.exe` or `VSTest@3` references remain
- [ ] Visual Studio / VS Code Test Explorer discovers and runs tests

---

## Future: .NET 10 SDK Upgrade
When you move to .NET 10 SDK, replace `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` with a `global.json` entry and drop the `--` separator:
```json
{ "test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" } }
```

## Rollback
Remove `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` from `Directory.Build.props`, restore `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` + `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and revert pipeline to `VSTest@3`.

## References
- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
- [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)
